meta.title: Property and Casualty Claims
meta.catalog: Guidewire ClaimCenter — Property & Casualty Best-Practice Catalog

steps.activity: FNOL received via phone, portal, app, or mail
steps.output: Claim ID assigned; loss date/time, circumstances and contact details recorded
Policy active at loss date; peril covered; deductible and sum insured confirmed; prior claims noted

steps.activity: Initial loss triage and priority assignment
Claim registered with reserve and acknowledgement
steps.output: Full record created; reserve estimate set; acknowledgement within 1 business day
Assignment by type, complexity and workload; adjuster contacts claimant within 2 days
Inspection within 5 days (standard) / 48h (urgent); digital photos for minor losses
Scope of damage; repair estimate or liability assessment; photos attached
Automated fraud score; flagged claims suspended and transferred to SIU within 1 day
Loss less deductible; ACV/RCV per policy basis; co-insurance check for property
Written itemised offer; claimant given 10 business days to accept or dispute
Bank transfer or cheque within 5 business days; remittance advice issued
Status 'Settled'; reserve adjusted to final paid amount; GL updated in SAP
Third-party liability established; recovery potential assessed; assignment of rights obtained
Demand letter to at-fault party/insurer; timeline tracked; Legal for contested recoveries
Total-loss vehicles and salvageable property sold via approved agent; proceeds reduce loss cost

swim_phases.name: FNOL & registration
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · notice to reserve
swim_phases.note: Multi-channel FNOL is registered with coverage verified and a reserve set at registration; fraud-flagged claims route to SIU.

swim_phases.name: Appraisal
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · assign, inspect, report
swim_phases.note: Claims are assigned and inspected (on-site or desktop) with external assessors for major losses; all claims are fraud-screened in parallel.

swim_phases.name: Adjustment & settlement
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–13 · calculate to close
swim_phases.note: Settlements are calculated, approved per authority and paid within five days of acceptance; disputes route to the Claims Manager.

swim_phases.name: Subrogation & salvage
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 14–16 · recover loss cost
swim_phases.note: Third-party recoveries and salvage sales reduce ultimate loss cost; contested recoveries are escalated to Legal.

items.name: Multi-channel FNOL
items.detail: Phone, portal, app and mail FNOL with Claim ID aligns with ClaimCenter FNOL intake.

items.name: Coverage verification
items.detail: Policy-active, peril, deductible and sum-insured verification via PAS aligns with ClaimCenter coverage check.

items.name: Loss triage & priority
items.detail: Standard/urgent/major/fraud triage aligns with ClaimCenter segmentation and routing.

items.name: Reserve at registration
items.detail: Initial reserve set when the claim is registered aligns with ClaimCenter reserving discipline.

items.name: Adjuster assignment
items.detail: Assignment by type, complexity and workload with a 2-day contact SLA aligns with ClaimCenter assignment.

items.name: AI photo-based damage estimation
items.detail: Digital photos are accepted but estimates are manual. ClaimCenter integrates AI photo-based estimation for fast, consistent repair costing on motor and property.

items.name: Inspection & loss documentation
items.detail: Inspection SLAs with scoped damage reports and attached evidence align with ClaimCenter assessment.

items.name: Fraud screening & analytics
items.detail: Automated fraud scoring and SIU referral are present, but without network/link analytics across claims and parties recommended by the catalog.

items.name: Settlement calculation
items.detail: ACV/RCV with deductible and co-insurance checks aligns with ClaimCenter settlement evaluation.

items.name: Authority & approval
items.detail: Tiered settlement authority (adjuster/manager/CCO) aligns with ClaimCenter authority management.

items.name: Touchless / STP settlement
items.detail: Even simple, low-value claims follow the full adjuster path. ClaimCenter offers straight-through, self-service settlement for clear minor claims, cutting cycle time and cost.

items.name: Managed-repair vendor network
items.detail: Repairs are authorised ad hoc; there is no preferred/managed-repair network with direct-repair and guarantees as the catalog recommends, raising indemnity cost.

items.name: Predictive reserving & analytics
items.detail: Reserves are set on adjuster judgement with a variance KPI. ClaimCenter uses predictive reserving and large-loss/litigation propensity analytics for accuracy and early escalation.

items.name: Payment & closure
items.detail: Payment within 5 days, closure and reserve release with GL update align with ClaimCenter financials.

items.name: Subrogation & recovery
items.detail: Subrogation identification, demand and Legal handling align with ClaimCenter recovery management.

items.name: Salvage management
items.detail: Salvage sale via approved agents with proceeds reducing loss cost aligns with ClaimCenter salvage.

critical_missing.title: AI photo-based damage estimation
critical_missing.detail: Digital photos are accepted but estimates are manual. The catalog integrates AI photo-based estimating for fast, consistent repair costing and lower leakage on motor and property.

critical_missing.title: Touchless / straight-through settlement
critical_missing.detail: Simple, low-value claims still follow the full adjuster path. The catalog provides straight-through, self-service settlement for clear minor claims, cutting cycle time and cost.

critical_missing.title: Managed-repair vendor network
critical_missing.detail: Repairs are authorised ad hoc with no preferred/managed-repair network, direct-repair or guarantees, raising indemnity cost and lengthening cycle time.

critical_missing.title: Predictive reserving & loss analytics
critical_missing.detail: Reserves rely on adjuster judgement; the catalog uses predictive reserving and large-loss/litigation propensity analytics for accuracy and early escalation.

control_bars.name: FNOL & registration
control_bars.note: Multi-channel FNOL with reserve at registration

control_bars.name: Damage estimation
control_bars.note: Manual estimating; no AI photo estimation

control_bars.name: Fraud & SIU
control_bars.note: Scoring and SIU; no network/link analytics

control_bars.name: Touchless settlement
control_bars.note: Full adjuster path even for minor claims

remediations.title: Integrate AI photo-based damage estimation
remediations.detail: Add AI estimating on motor and property photos for fast, consistent repair costing and reduced leakage. Effort: 6 weeks (vendor integration).

remediations.title: Enable touchless / STP settlement for minor claims
remediations.detail: Auto-adjudicate clear, low-value claims with self-service settlement, routing only exceptions to adjusters. Effort: 6 weeks.

remediations.title: Stand up a managed-repair vendor network
remediations.detail: Establish a preferred/managed-repair network with direct-repair, agreed rates and guarantees to control indemnity and cycle time. Effort: 8 weeks.

remediations.title: Add predictive reserving & loss analytics
remediations.detail: Introduce predictive reserving and large-loss/litigation propensity analytics for accurate reserves and early escalation. Effort: 5 weeks.

remediations.title: Add claimant self-service tracking & SIU analytics
remediations.detail: Provide a claimant status-tracking portal and extend fraud screening with network/link analytics. Effort: 5 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Claim ID; loss details and contact captured
Active policy, peril, deductible and sum insured confirmed
Triage augmented by large-loss/litigation propensity scoring
Predictive reserve set; acknowledgement within 1 business day
Clear minor claims routed to straight-through settlement; others assigned to adjuster
AI estimate from claimant/assessor photos for motor and property
On-site for major losses; AI/desktop for minor; SLAs unchanged
Scope, estimate and evidence attached to the claim file
Fraud scoring plus network/link analytics across claims and parties

opt_steps.activity: Touchless settlement for clear minor claims
opt_steps.output: Straight-through self-service settlement for clear, low-value claims
Repairs directed to a managed network at agreed rates with guarantees
Itemised offer; payment within 5 business days of acceptance
Recovery opportunities surfaced by analytics; assignment of rights obtained
Demand to at-fault party/insurer; Legal for contested recoveries
Salvage sold via approved agents; proceeds reduce loss cost

opt_swim_phases.note: v4.0 augments triage and reserving with predictive analytics for accuracy and early escalation.

opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–9 · v4.0 adds AI estimation
opt_swim_phases.note: v4.0 adds AI photo-based estimation for fast costing and extends fraud screening with network/link analytics.

opt_swim_phases.name: Settlement & repair

opt_swim_phases.note: v4.0 settles clear minor claims straight-through and directs repairs to a managed network at agreed rates.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire ClaimCenter P&C Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v4.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-012. Alignment to the Guidewire ClaimCenter P&C catalog increased from 74% to 93%. Critical additions: AI photo-based damage estimation, touchless/straight-through settlement for minor claims, a managed-repair vendor network, predictive reserving and loss analytics, and SIU network analytics.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the ClaimCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional automation or controls

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the end-to-end process for receiving, triaging, appraising, adjusting and settling property and casualty claims, including subrogation and salvage. Version 4.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire ClaimCenter P&C Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 93% alignment (up from 74% in v3.1).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to all personal and commercial property damage claims, casualty and third-party liability claims, FNOL across channels, and subrogation and salvage recovery.
Scope extended: AI estimation, touchless settlement and managed-repair now apply to eligible motor and property claims.
This procedure does NOT cover medical claims (SOP-INS-011), life claims (SOP-INS-013), or reinsurance claim notifications (SOP-RE-001).

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Registers FNOL; verifies coverage; triages; assigns; closes the file.
Assesses damage/liability; uses AI estimation; directs managed repair; settles within authority; identifies subrogation.
Approves above adjuster authority; oversees predictive reserving; manages litigation referrals.
On-site assessment for major losses; independent estimates.
Investigates flagged claims; runs network/link analytics; recommends action.

sections.title: 5. Estimation, Settlement & Repair Controls (New Section — v4.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the ClaimCenter catalog. AI estimation, touchless settlement and managed-repair now govern eligible motor and property claims.
Self-service settlement for clear minor claims
Direct-repair at agreed rates with guarantee
Reserve and large-loss propensity from models

sections.title: 6. Process Steps
blocks: On-site for major; AI/desktop for minor
Touchless settlement for clear minor claims

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Claimant disputes in writing within 10 business days; dispute logged in the Claims System.
Claims Manager reviews within 5 business days; revised offer or maintained position communicated in writing.
Unresolved after 30 days: claimant advised of external dispute resolution (FIDREC / MAS scheme).
Network/link analytics now strengthen fraud detection across related claims and parties.
SIU takes custody of the claim; settlement is suspended.
Investigation completed within 20 business days; Claims Manager decides to reject, settle or refer.
Rejected fraud claims logged and submitted to the industry fraud database.
Total loss declared when repair cost exceeds 75% of pre-accident market value.
Market valuation obtained from an approved independent valuer; settlement on market value less salvage.
Finance encumbrance checked; payment directed to the financier first where applicable.
Claims Manager notified immediately; CCO briefed for losses above SGD 250,000.
External loss adjusters deployed; a dedicated major-loss team assigned.
Reinsurance notification triggered per SOP-RE-001 above treaty retention.

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v4.0: AI estimation, touchless settlement, managed-repair, predictive reserving and SIU network analytics.
Reserve at registration: predictive reserve set when the claim is created.
AI estimation: consistent photo-based repair costing reduces leakage.
Touchless settlement: clear minor claims settled straight-through with self-service.
Managed repair: indemnity controlled via a network with agreed rates and guarantees.
Fraud analytics: scoring plus network/link analysis across claims and parties.
Segregation of duties: assessor ≠ settlement approver ≠ payment.
Immutable audit trail: FNOL, estimate, settlement, payment and recovery events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Updated SGD authority thresholds; added total loss motor protocol; aligned to reinsurance notification SOP
OPTIMISED: AI photo-based estimation; touchless/STP settlement; managed-repair network; predictive reserving & analytics; SIU network analytics; catalog alignment 74% → 93%
